title: psoralea polyphylla
intro: Psoralea polyphylla, also known as bishop's weed, is a perennial plant native to the Indian subcontinent and Southeast Asia, belonging to the Apiaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to stimulate the immune system and exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Psoralea polyphylla include its potential to improve digestion, boost the immune system, and reduce the risk of certain cancers. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its effectiveness and safety as a medicinal agent are still being researched and studied.
